payload.title: Nominal newtypes at API boundaries
payload.description:
Forbid more than one adjacent same-typed parameter on a pub fn unless wrapped in distinct newtypes (Width(u32), PostId(u64)); provide a lowering/derive that makes a newtype a one-liner.

Why frontend-only / Clippy-proof: named args fix swap VISIBILITY at the call site, but area(width: w, height: h) still accepts two raw u32s — an agent that computed the values into the wrong variables upstream is not caught. This kills the units / ID-mixup class (post_id passed where user_id expected, both u64) — value constructed wrong, not ordered wrong. Most named-args-shaped idea in the tier.
